violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH. Application was filled on in an envelope. PIC to mail or drop off application by tomorrow. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH. Application printed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024

Potatoes and hollandaise sauce was observed held below 135 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Both items recently came out of temperature. Both items were reheated. Potatoes shall not be held on the counter. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024

Various food were observed held at 46-48 degrees Fahrenheit in the prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Owner called technician at the time of inspection. Food was moved in from reach-in unit that was holding temperature less than 4 hours prior. Food was moved to ice in the prep unit. Ice must be used as a temporary set up for today only. All food not in use moved to reach-in unit. Prep unit to not be used if unable to maintain a temperature of 41 or less. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024
Employees' medicines were not stored to prevent the contamination of food, equipment, or utensils. Medication was relocated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Hot sauce and potatoes/home fries were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Items were relocated to freezer to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024

The prep unit was observed unable to hold a temperature of 41 degrees or less at the time of inspection. Unit to be taken out of service after today if unable to hold temperature. PIC to monitor temperature of unit AND food. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024

The pre-flush spray hose at the 3-bay sink is hanging below the flood rim. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 180 degrees Fahrenheit per manufacturer specs for dishwasher was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. PIC will wash and rinse in dishwasher and sanitize in sanitizer compartment of 3 bay sink. Service technician called to come into service dishwasher. PIC will send picture of proper sanitizing temperature. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/09/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, beans was placed hot in the refrigerator in a plastic container greater than four inches deep. Beans were moved into a smaller stainless steal pan and placed in the freezer for continued cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/09/2023
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the kitchen area. New Violation. Correct By: 08/09/2023

The facility is lacking a mop sink. At least one mop sink or one curbed cleaning facility equipped with a floor drain must be provided and conveniently located for the cleaning of mops or similar wet floor cleaning tools and for the disposal of mop water and similar liquid waste. PIC contacting plumber to have one installed in the basement and will send picture to inspector. New Violation. Correct By: 08/23/2023
